name: La Mariana Sailing Club
tagline: Hawaii's original tiki bar since 1957 — hidden in an industrial marina with museum-worthy tiki artifacts and strong mai tais.
talkStory: Hawaii's original tiki bar since 1957, hidden in an industrial marina you'd never find unless you knew it was there. The interior is a living museum of tiki culture — puffer fish lamps from the original Trader Vic's, rattan chairs from Don the Beachcomber, and hand-carved tikis from the old Kon Tiki Room. The mai tais are strong and the live music on Friday evenings sets a wonderfully retro mood. Founder Annette La Mariana originally charged $2 membership and 50-cent boat slips.
